title: Getting started with edr4r
text: client just stores connection settings (base URL, user agent, timeout, retry policy). Pass verbose = TRUE echo every request URL, headers = attach auth tokens.
code: library(edr4r)  # Local pygeoapi dev server; swap for the hosted URL in production. wwdh <- edr_client("http://localhost:5005") wwdh

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: data_queries tells EDR query types collection supports. see parameters collection accepts:
code: collections <- edr_collections(wwdh) collections[, c("id", "title", "data_queries")] q <- edr_queryables(wwdh, "snotel-edr") names(q$properties)

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: filters, locations returns GeoJSON FeatureCollection, edr4r promotes sf object (sf installed): can pre-filter list spatially:
code: rise_locs <- edr_locations(wwdh, "rise-edr") rise_locs  library(sf) plot(st_geometry(rise_locs), pch = 19, cex = 0.4) edr_locations(wwdh, "snotel-edr", bbox = c(-120, 39, -118, 41))

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: Add location_id datetime get CoverageJSON, flatten : tibble long tidy — ready dplyr/ggplot2:
code: resp <- edr_location(   wwdh, "rise-edr",   location_id    = 247,   datetime       = "2020-01-01/2020-12-31",   parameter_name = c("storage", "elevation") )  df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp) head(df) library(ggplot2) ggplot(df, aes(datetime, value, colour = parameter)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ parameter, scales = "free_y")

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: cube takes bounding box; area takes polygon (WKT, lon/lat matrix, sf polygon):
code: cube <- edr_cube(   wwdh, "snotel-edr",   bbox           = c(-120, 39, -118, 41),   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-03-31",   parameter_name = "WTEQ" ) covjson_to_tibble(cube)  ring <- matrix(c(-109, 47, -104, 47, -104, 49, -109, 49), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) area <- edr_area(wwdh, "rise-edr", coords = ring, datetime = "2022-01-01/..") covjson_to_tibble(area)

what: 6. Other formats and the escape hatch
title: Getting started with edr4r
code: # CSV straight from the server edr_location(wwdh, "snotel-edr", "1175", datetime = "2010-01-01/..", format = "csv")  # Anything not wrapped by a helper: edr_request(wwdh, "collections/rise-edr", format = "json")

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: datetime accepts "start/end", open intervals ("2020-01-01/.."), length-2 character vector. parameter_name character vector; sent single comma-separated parameter-name query parameter. Station identifiers reserved characters (e.g. AWDB triplets like "1185:CO:SNTL") handled . WWDH implements locations, cube, area. position, radius, trajectory, corridor verbs exist client spec completeness servers, error collections implement . ```

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {edr4r: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)},   author = {Kyle Onda},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r}, }

what: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: R client OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR) services, built Western Water Datahub (WWDH) pygeoapi deployment usable compliant EDR server. handles boring parts — URL construction, comma-separated parameter lists, WKT coordinate encoding, retries, content negotiation — parses responses tidy structures: CoverageJSON → long tibble (one row per coverage × parameter × time-step), via covjson_to_tibble(). GeoJSON → sf object, via geojson_to_sf().

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: local development: Requires R >= 4.1. sf package optional recommended (used turn location lists GeoJSON spatial objects).
code: # from GitHub (recommended) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("ksonda/edr4r")  # or # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ksonda/edr4r") git clone https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r.git cd edr4r R -e 'devtools::install()'

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
code: library(edr4r)  # Point at a running WWDH server: local dev, or the hosted instance # at "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app". wwdh <- edr_client("http://localhost:5005")  # What collections are available? edr_collections(wwdh) #> # A tibble: 7 × 7 #>   id                 title                         description  extent_bbox crs   data_queries links #>   <chr>              <chr>                         <chr>        <list>      <chr> <list>       <list> #> 1 rise-edr           USBR RISE                     ...          <dbl [4]>   ...   <chr [3]>    ... #> 2 snotel-edr         USDA SNOTEL                   ...          <dbl [4]>   ...   <chr [3]>    ... #> ...

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
code: locs <- edr_locations(wwdh, "rise-edr") locs                       # sf POINTs with station attributes plot(sf::st_geometry(locs))

what: Pull a time series for one location (CoverageJSON → tibble)
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
code: resp <- edr_location(   wwdh, "rise-edr",   location_id    = 247,   datetime       = "2020-01-01/2020-12-31",   parameter_name = c("storage", "elevation") )  df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp) df #> # A tibble: 732 × 9 #>   coverage_id parameter parameter_label   unit       datetime                x     y     z value #>   <chr>       <chr>     <chr>             <chr>      <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 247         storage   Reservoir Storage acre-feet  2020-01-01 00:00:00 -104.  40.4    NA  100.5 #> ...

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
code: # Everything in a bbox over a date range cube <- edr_cube(   wwdh, "snotel-edr",   bbox           = c(-120, 39, -118, 41),   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-03-31",   parameter_name = "WTEQ" ) covjson_to_tibble(cube)  # Inside an arbitrary polygon (matrix of lon/lat, an sf polygon, or WKT) ring <- matrix(   c(-109, 47, -104, 47, -104, 49, -109, 49),   ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE ) area <- edr_area(wwdh, "rise-edr", coords = ring, datetime = "2022-01-01/..") covjson_to_tibble(area)

what: Station triplets, CSV, and escape hatches
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
code: # AWDB forecast station triplets work as-is (encoded for you) edr_location(wwdh, "awdb-forecasts-edr", "1185:CO:SNTL", datetime = "2024-01-01/..")  # Ask the server for CSV instead of CovJSON edr_location(wwdh, "snotel-edr", "1175", datetime = "2010-01-01/..", format = "csv")  # Drop down to a raw request for anything not wrapped by a helper edr_request(wwdh, "collections/rise-edr/instances", format = "json")

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Note WWDH coverage: WWDH providers currently implement locations, cube, area (plus stub items). position, radius, trajectory, corridor part EDR spec supported client, return error collections don’t implement .

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Every query verb accepts standard EDR filters: datetime — ISO-8601 instant interval. Accepts "2020-01-01/2020-12-31", open interval "2020-01-01/..", length-2 character vector c("2020-01-01", "2020-12-31"). parameter_name — character vector parameter names; sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. Use edr_queryables() discover valid names. bbox — numeric length-4 (minx, miny, maxx, maxy) length-6 (z). coords — position/area/radius/trajectory/corridor: WKT string, numeric vector / 2-column matrix lon-lat, sf/sfc geometry. z, crs, limit — passed supplied. ... — extra query parameter forwarded verbatim.

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: MIT

what: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: Flattens CoverageJSON Coverage CoverageCollection long tibble one row per (coverage, parameter, time-step). Handles Point PointSeries domain types used station-based EDR providers, falls back general N-dimensional unrolling Grid-like domains.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
code: covjson_to_tibble(x, datetime_as_posix = TRUE)

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: x CoverageJSON object: either edr_response returned edr_location() / edr_area() / edr_cube() (etc.) format = "covjson", raw parsed list. datetime_as_posix TRUE (default), attempts parse time axis POSIXct (UTC). Falls back character failure.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: tibble columns coverage_id, parameter, parameter_label, unit, datetime, x, y, z, value. Columns absent source filled NA.

what: edr4r: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
title: edr4r: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
text: R client OGC API - EDR endpoints. Designed Western Water Datahub (WWDH) pygeoapi deployment, works compliant EDR service. Build client edr_client(), discover resources edr_collections(), query data edr_locations(), edr_area(), edr_cube(), edr_position(), related verbs. Parse responses covjson_to_tibble() geojson_to_sf().

title: edr4r: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
text: Maintainer: Western Water Datahub bwebb@lincolninst.edu

what: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
title: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
text: Calls GET /collections/{collection_id}/area WKT POLYGON coords parameter.

title: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
code: edr_area(   client,   collection_id,   coords,   datetime = NULL,   parameter_name = NULL,   z = NULL,   crs = NULL,   format = c("covjson", "json"),   ... )

title: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. coords WKT polygon string, matrix / data.frame (lon, lat) rows closed POLYGON. May also sf / sfc polygon sf installed. datetime ISO-8601 instant interval, e.g. "2024-01-01/2024-12-31" "2024-01-01/..". parameter_name Character vector parameter names filter . Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. z Vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format "covjson" (default), "geojson", "csv", "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: Builds reusable client object captures base URL OGC API - EDR service, default user-agent, HTTP options applied every request.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
code: edr_client(   base_url,   user_agent = NULL,   timeout = 60,   max_tries = 3,   headers = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: base_url Base URL EDR service, e.g. "http://localhost:5005" local WWDH deployment "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.dev" hosted instance. trailing slash optional. user_agent String sent User-Agent header. Defaults "edr4r/<version> (+https://github.com/internetofwater/WWDH)". timeout Request timeout seconds. Defaults 60. max_tries Maximum number attempts per request. client retries 408, 429, 5xx responses exponential backoff. Defaults 3. headers Named character vector extra headers attached every request (e.g. c(Authorization = "Bearer ...")). verbose TRUE, prints request URLs console made. Useful debugging.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: object class edr_client.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
code: wwdh <- edr_client("http://localhost:5005") wwdh #> <edr_client> #>   base_url:   <http://localhost:5005> #>   user_agent: edr4r/0.1.0 (+https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r) #>   timeout:    60s #>   max_tries:  3

title: Items (OGC API Features) helpers — edr_items
text: Many EDR servers expose OGC API Features /items endpoint alongside EDR queries. Western Water Datahub providers implement items thin stub used register collection EDR Features; non-trivial data usually obtained via EDR queries.

Additional query parameters passed verbatim. item_id Identifier single feature.

what: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: Generally need call directly: high-level verbs (edr_locations(), edr_area(), etc.) build path query string . Use edr_request() need hit bespoke path non-standard parameter.

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
code: edr_request(   client,   path,   query = list(),   format = c("json", "geojson", "covjson", "csv", "html", "raw"),   parse = TRUE )

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: client edr_client edr_client(). path Path base URL (without leading slash), e.g. "collections/rise-edr/locations". query Named list query parameters. Values may scalars vectors; vectors joined ",". NULL entries dropped. format Response format: one "json" (default), "geojson", "covjson", "csv", "html", "raw". Passed ?f= (except "covjson", sent ?f=json CoverageJSON Accept hint, since EDR servers return CovJSON via JSON). parse TRUE (default), parses JSON / GeoJSON / CovJSON bodies R structures. FALSE, returns raw httr2 response.

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: parsed body (list / tibble / sf) httr2_response parse = FALSE.

what: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
text: Convert GeoJSON EDR response sf object

title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
code: geojson_to_sf(x)

title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
text: x edr_response wrapping GeoJSON (e.g. edr_locations()) raw parsed GeoJSON list.

title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
text: sf object. Requires sf package. sf installed, returns tibble feature properties (without geometry) warns.

title: edr4r 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Discovery: edr_landing(), edr_conformance(), edr_collections(), edr_collection(), edr_queryables(). Query verbs: edr_locations() / edr_location(), edr_items() / edr_item(), edr_position(), edr_area(), edr_cube(), edr_radius(), edr_trajectory(), edr_corridor(). Low-level escape hatch: edr_request(). Parsers: covjson_to_tibble() CoverageJSON, geojson_to_sf() GeoJSON.
